description: Palatalization of bilabial stop before front vowels or palatal consonants, yielding a soft bilabial [bʲ] or [bʲ] allophone. Common in Eastern Armenian dialects.
sources: Vaux, B. (2015). _The Phonology of Armenian_. Oxford: Oxford University Press.
Dum-Tragut, J. (2009). _Armenian: Modern Eastern Armenian_. Hamburg: Helmut Buske Verlag.

languages.description: Palatalized allophone [bʲ] appears before front vowels /i, e/ and after palatal approximants.
